title: [Question] How to let podman use the local image
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

/kind feature

**Description**

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. There is `registry:2.7.0` image in the local.
```yaml
[cloud-user@preserve-olm-env ~]$ podman images|grep -E 'registry.*2.7.0'
docker.io/library/registry                                                                       2.7.0            33fbbf4a24e5  3 years ago    24.8 MB
```
2. But, podman still pulls it from the remote registry. My question is how to let podman use the local image, thanks!
```yaml
[cloud-user@preserve-olm-env jian]$ podman run -d --name registry_native_auth -p 5000:5000 -v /data/jian/auth:/auth -e "REGISTRY_AUTH=htpasswd" -e "REGISTRY_AUTH_HTPASSWD_REALM=Registry Realm" -e REGISTRY_AUTH_HTPASSWD_PATH=/auth/htpasswd -v /data/jian/certs:/certs -e REGISTRY_HTTP_TLS_CERTIFICATE=/certs/domain.crt -e REGISTRY_HTTP_TLS_KEY=/certs/domain.key --privileged registry:2.7.0
Resolved "registry" as an alias (/etc/containers/registries.conf.d/000-shortnames.conf)
Trying to pull docker.io/library/registry:2.7.0...
WARN[0030] failed, retrying in 1s ... (1/3). Error: initializing source docker://registry:2.7.0: reading manifest 2.7.0 in docker.io/library/registry: toomanyrequests: You have reached your pull rate limit. You may increase the limit by authenticating and upgrading: https://www.docker.com/increase-rate-limit 
...
```

3.

**Describe the results you received:**
Podman stills pull the remote image even if it exists in the local.

**Describe the results you expected:**


**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
[cloud-user@preserve-olm-env ~]$ podman version
Version:      3.0.2-dev
API Version:  3.0.0
Go Version:   go1.15.13
Built:        Tue Jun  8 03:52:06 2021
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```
(paste your output here)
```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
(paste your output here)
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md)**


Yes/No

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

comments.body: @jianzhangbjz I think `podman run --pull missing ... ... ` should solve your use-case ? It will pull only if `image` is missing from the `local` store.

There is something else going on.

@jianzhangbjz, if possible, please share the _entire_ requested information, especially `podman info --debug`.

@flouthoc Thanks! But, the `--pull` default value is `missing`. 
```yaml
      --pull string                              Pull image before creating ("always"|"missing"|"never") (default "missing")

[cloud-user@preserve-olm-env2 jian]$ sudo podman run --pull missing -d --name registry_native_auth -p 5000:5000 -v /data/jian/auth:/auth -e "REGISTRY_AUTH=htpasswd" -e "REGISTRY_AUTH_HTPASSWD_REALM=Registry Realm" -e REGISTRY_AUTH_HTPASSWD_PATH=/auth/htpasswd -v /data/jian/certs:/certs -e REGISTRY_HTTP_TLS_CERTIFICATE=/certs/domain.crt -e REGISTRY_HTTP_TLS_KEY=/certs/domain.key --privileged docker.io/library/registry:2.7.0
Trying to pull docker.io/library/registry:2.7.0...
WARN[0030] failed, retrying in 1s ... (1/3). Error: initializing source docker://registry:2.7.0: reading manifest 2.7.0 in docker.io/library/registry: toomanyrequests: You have reached your pull rate limit. You may increase the limit by authenticating and upgrading: https://www.docker.com/increase-rate-limit 
```

@vrothberg  Thanks! I used a new version have a try, but still get this issue. Details:
```yaml
[cloud-user@preserve-olm-env2 jian]$ podman images
REPOSITORY                  TAG         IMAGE ID      CREATED        SIZE
docker.io/library/registry  2.7.0       7a0630bf7af7  6 minutes ago  24.8 MB

[cloud-user@preserve-olm-env2 jian]$ sudo podman run -d --name registry_native_auth -p 5000:5000 -v /data/jian/auth:/auth -e "REGISTRY_AUTH=htpasswd" -e "REGISTRY_AUTH_HTPASSWD_REALM=Registry Realm" -e REGISTRY_AUTH_HTPASSWD_PATH=/auth/htpasswd -v /data/jian/certs:/certs -e REGISTRY_HTTP_TLS_CERTIFICATE=/certs/domain.crt -e REGISTRY_HTTP_TLS_KEY=/certs/domain.key --privileged docker.io/library/registry:2.7.0
Trying to pull docker.io/library/registry:2.7.0...

WARN[0030] failed, retrying in 1s ... (1/3). Error: initializing source docker://registry:2.7.0: reading manifest 2.7.0 in docker.io/library/registry: toomanyrequests: You have reached your pull rate limit. You may increase the limit by authenticating and upgrading: https://www.docker.com/increase-rate-limit

^C[cloud-user@preserve-olm-env2 jian]$ podman info --debug

  cpus: 8

  idMappings:

  kernel: 4.18.0-287.el8.dt4.x86_64

      Version: |-
        fusermount3 version: 3.2.1
        fuse-overlayfs: version 1.8
        FUSE library version 3.2.1
        using FUSE kernel interface version 7.26
  graphRoot: /home/cloud-user/.local/share/containers/storage

  imageStore:

  APIVersion: 3.4.2

  Version: 3.4.2
Thanks, @jianzhangbjz.  `podman info` looks good.  Could you rerun with `podman --log-level=debug` and share that output?

Sure, as follows,
```yaml
[cloud-user@preserve-olm-env2 jian]$ sudo podman run --log-level=debug -d --name registry_native_auth -p 5000:5000 -v /data/jian/auth:/auth -e "REGISTRY_AUTH=htpasswd" -e "REGISTRY_AUTH_HTPASSWD_REALM=Registry Realm" -e REGISTRY_AUTH_HTPASSWD_PATH=/auth/htpasswd -v /data/jian/certs:/certs -e REGISTRY_HTTP_TLS_CERTIFICATE=/certs/domain.crt -e REGISTRY_HTTP_TLS_KEY=/certs/domain.key --privileged docker.io/library/registry:2.7.0 
INFO[0000] podman filtering at log level debug          
DEBU[0000] Called run.PersistentPreRunE(podman run --log-level=debug -d --name registry_native_auth -p 5000:5000 -v /data/jian/auth:/auth -e REGISTRY_AUTH=htpasswd -e REGISTRY_AUTH_HTPASSWD_REALM=Registry Realm -e REGISTRY_AUTH_HTPASSWD_PATH=/auth/htpasswd -v /data/jian/certs:/certs -e REGISTRY_HTTP_TLS_CERTIFICATE=/certs/domain.crt -e REGISTRY_HTTP_TLS_KEY=/certs/domain.key --privileged docker.io/library/registry:2.7.0) 
DEBU[0000] Merged system config "/usr/share/containers/containers.conf"

DEBU[0000] Initializing event backend file

INFO[0000] Setting parallel job count to 25

Trying to pull docker.io/library/registry:2.7.0...
DEBU[0000] Copying source image //registry:2.7.0 to destination image [overlay@/var/lib/containers/storage+/run/containers/storage:overlay.mountopt=nodev,metacopy=on]docker.io/library/registry:2.7.0

DEBU[0000] Too many requests to https://registry-1.docker.io/v2/library/registry/manifests/2.7.0: sleeping for 2.000000 seconds before next attempt

DEBU[0002] Too many requests to https://registry-1.docker.io/v2/library/registry/manifests/2.7.0: sleeping for 4.000000 seconds before next attempt 
DEBU[0006] GET https://registry-1.docker.io/v2/library/registry/manifests/2.7.0 
DEBU[0006] Too many requests to https://registry-1.docker.io/v2/library/registry/manifests/2.7.0: sleeping for 8.000000 seconds before next attempt
...
```

@jianzhangbjz, the logs indicate that the image has not been found in the local storage.

Could it be that the `podman images` is executed as the rootless user but the container is run with `sudo` (i.e., rootless)?
@vrothberg Yes, I think so, thanks for your checking. Close it.
Glad we could resolve the issue, @jianzhangbjz.
